title: Estimating a Sum by Rounding to the Nearest Ten: 47 + 34

steps.subgoalLabel: Round each number to the nearest ten
steps.instruction: Look at the ones digit to decide whether to round up or down.
steps.work: 47: ones digit is 7 (5 or more → round up) → 50. 34: ones digit is 4 (less than 5 → round down) → 30.
steps.explanation: When the ones digit is 5 or more, round up to the next ten. Otherwise, round down.
Adding rounded numbers is quick mental math.
The exact answer is 81, so the estimate of 80 is very close!

title: Estimating a Sum by Rounding to the Nearest Hundred: 347 + 562

steps.subgoalLabel: Round each number to the nearest hundred
steps.instruction: Look at the tens digit to decide.
steps.work: 347: tens digit is 4 (less than 5) → 300. 562: tens digit is 6 (5 or more) → 600.
steps.explanation: To round to the nearest hundred, check the tens digit.
Adding hundreds is simple mental math.
The exact answer is 909. Estimation helps you check if your exact answer is reasonable.
